behaviors.description: The evaluate-stage command stores grounding into a dedicated `grounding` artifact, not the `plan` artifact.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the handoff between evaluate-stage logic and artifact persistence keys. A unit test of key selection can miss real regressions in command wiring/serialization, while no OS/network/LLM behavior is required.
behaviors.test_description: Run evaluate-stage end-to-end in a local harness with real artifact store implementation (e.g., temp filesystem/DB). Seed an issue, execute evaluate, then assert: `grounding` artifact exists with expected content shape; `plan` artifact is unchanged/not newly written by evaluate.

behaviors.description: The implement-stage flow reads grounding when available and still writes its execution output to `plan`.
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on interaction of artifact read path (`grounding`) and write path (`plan`) across modules. Mocking either side can hide key-mapping/state bugs. Real external infra is not required for this failure mode.
behaviors.test_description: Preload artifacts with a `grounding` payload and optional existing `plan`. Execute implement-stage through real orchestration and artifact store. Assert implement consumed grounding (e.g., grounding markers influence produced plan) and wrote output only to `plan` slot while preserving other artifact namespaces.

behaviors.description: Deep-dive no longer overwrites `plan` as a redundant side effect when diagnosis already exists in issue content.
behaviors.justification: The bug appears at the boundary between issue-content diagnosis detection and artifact-write side effects. A pure unit check of the predicate is insufficient to catch unintended write calls in the wired flow.
behaviors.test_description: Create an issue fixture containing diagnosis text. Run deep-dive stage with real stage wiring and artifact store. Assert no write/update to `plan` occurred (timestamp/version unchanged) and deep-dive outputs only expected non-plan artifacts/logging.

behaviors.description: In a full multi-step agent run (`deep-dive -> write-plan/evaluate -> implement`), the final implementation plan reflects grounding produced earlier in the run.
behaviors.justification: This is explicitly an end-to-end multi-step agent chain where correctness depends on cross-step propagation of generated grounding into later generated plan output. Single-step tests can pass while sequential agent behavior fails.
behaviors.test_description: Execute a real chained run with all three stages in sequence on one task context. Capture grounding produced at evaluate stage, then verify final implement `plan` materially incorporates that grounding (traceable constraints/facts present). Assert artifact lineage across steps remains consistent.

behaviors.description: When grounding is absent, implement falls back safely and still emits a valid plan artifact without corrupting other stored artifacts.
behaviors.justification: Key risk is fallback behavior under missing-artifact state plus write isolation across artifact slots. This is a module interaction/state-management failure, best caught with real local store integration.
behaviors.test_description: Run implement-stage with no `grounding` artifact but with other unrelated artifacts pre-seeded. Assert implement succeeds, emits syntactically/structurally valid `plan`, and leaves non-plan artifacts byte-for-byte unchanged.
